repo_prior_art.cite: none; closest catalogue entries stat_policy_regret_margin_overlap and stat_dose_response_minimax have different estimands and risk criteria

published_prior_art.cite: WuWang2021 and XuGuo2026 give optimal-value inference; WhitehouseAusternSyrgkanis2025 is a closer omitted smoothing-based optimal-policy-value inference comparator, though none located states this finite-union expected-length result

repo_prior_art.cite: none; no catalogue entry studies honest-interval length for an optimal-policy value

published_prior_art.cite: CaiLow2004 derives ordered/between-class modulus expected-length lower bounds for linear Gaussian functionals, not this causal maximum
LuedtkeVanderLaan2016 treats exceptional-law nonregularity and root-n optimal-value inference under its conditions; the displayed plateau calculation is a specialization
CaiLow2004, XuGuo2026, WhitehouseAusternSyrgkanis2025, and WuWang2021 do not state a margin--tie--smoothness causal ordered-modulus adaptation diagram

structure_flags.label: project headline / thm:uniform-honesty-upper-length
structure_flags.one_line: The strongest concrete kernel is still a to-prove theorem rather than a conjecture or OEQ; after mathematical repair, make the claimed honest-length frontier an explicit conjecture and retain the OEQ as its residual.

novelty_flags.label: thm:uniform-honesty-upper-length
novelty_flags.one_line: The survey omits WhitehouseAusternSyrgkanis2025, a closer softmax-smoothing optimal-policy-value inference result that must be distinguished from the proposed finite-union honest expected-length target.

soundness_flags.label: thm:uniform-honesty-upper-length and thm:two-regime-reduction
soundness_flags.one_line: The declared interval has length 4w_{\widehat j,\widehat h}\ge4\sqrt{v_0}z_{1-\gamma/(4J\sum_\ell|\mathcal H_{\ell,n}|)}/\sqrt n, and the quantile diverges as the grids grow, so no n-independent D_{j,\gamma} can yield the asserted root-n upper length when r_{j,n}=o(n^{-1/2}).

soundness_flags.label: thm:two-regime-reduction
soundness_flags.one_line: Under ass:admissible-index, \alpha_j\le1/s_j makes s_j/(2s_j+d)>(\alpha_j+2)/(4(\alpha_j+1)) impossible for every d\ge1, so the advertised separated root-n sanity regime is vacuous on every declared stratum.

soundness_flags.label: def:score-family / lem:wavelet-rate / thm:uniform-honesty-upper-length
soundness_flags.one_line: The wavelet resolution is written 2^{r_{j,n}} although r_{j,n} is already the noninteger upper-length rate; introduce a distinct integer resolution index, since the named learner is otherwise not defined.

source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The article proves a necessary-and-sufficient pathwise-differentiability condition for the optimal value and describes root-n confidence intervals including at non-pathwise-differentiable laws.
The article proposes a smoothed robust estimator and resampling-based asymptotically accurate inference for both the optimal-regime parameter and its value function.

The preprint develops adaptive smoothing for valid optimal-regime-value inference under nonregularity and states an attained asymptotic-variance lower bound within robust asymptotically linear unbiased estimators.
The article concerns linear functionals in Gaussian nonparametric models and gives expected-length lower bounds through between-class and ordered moduli.
The paper combines a margin assumption, Hölder regression class, and strong density assumption to obtain plug-in classification rates.
The preprint gives smoothing-based inference for optimal-policy values, claiming root-n convergence without parametric outcome-regression restrictions or strong margin assumptions.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the Bonferroni half-width by a simultaneous critical-value construction with a proved bounded critical value, or explicitly include z_{1-\gamma/(4J\sum|\mathcal H|)}/\sqrt n in every length claim and revise the separated-regime label accordingly.
Split admissibility by regime: retain \alpha\le1/s only where the nonseparated plateau witness needs it, and permit separated constant-contrast strata satisfying the displayed root-n inequality.
Rename the wavelet level to an integer q_{j,n}, state its exact rounding rule, and propagate the resulting approximation and stochastic constants into b_j(h,n).
Add Whitehouse, Austern, and Syrgkanis (2025), then formulate a theorem-level distinction between its smoothing inference and the proposed finite-union honest expected-length frontier.

recommended_next_step: Return to Stage -1 for a mathematical redesign: repair the deterministic width-rate contradiction and the vacuous root-n regime before reassessing novelty.
